research.summary: HVAC ranking factors are heavily category-weighted — GBP category choice + services + attributes account for up to 50% of ranking impact in this vertical. Demand is bi-seasonal (cooling Mar-Apr peak, heating Sep-Oct peak); content must be indexed 60-90 days BEFORE peak to capture demand surges. Primary category 'HVAC contractor' is canonical.
research.recommendedPrimaryCategoryReasoning: Empirically dominant in Culver City top-5 (9/9 use 'HVAC contractor'). Canonical and unambiguous.

topRankingFactors.benchmark: Must be 'HVAC contractor'. Narrower variants limit eligibility.
All 15 attributes filled. Individual service items, not paragraph summaries.
Set accurate attributes — Google weights these for filter matching.
Cooling content live Mar-Apr (60-90d before summer); heating content live Sep-Oct.
Sustained weekly review flow > large historical totals.
Mention annual maintenance plans on GBP description + website — high-LTV differentiator.
topRankingFactors.source: Agile Solutions, ALM Corp HVAC SEO

commonPitfalls: Using narrower 'Air conditioning contractor' or 'Heating contractor' as PRIMARY — limits eligibility for combined HVAC searches
Service section using paragraph summaries instead of individual service items
No seasonal content cadence (waiting for demand to spike before posting)
Missing 'Open 24 hours' attribute when emergency service is offered
No maintenance plan offering visible (high-LTV miss)
Stale GBP photos (no fresh seasonal images)
research.titleTagExample: HVAC Contractor in Culver City, CA | Lions Heating & Air
research.h1Example: HVAC Contractor in Culver City — AC, Heating, 24/7 Service
research.metaDescExample: Licensed HVAC contractor in Culver City, CA. AC & heating repair, installation, maintenance plans, 24/7 emergency service. Free estimates. Call (310) 555-0400.
citationProfileTargets: Carrier Factory Authorized Dealer directory (if applicable)
voiceoverHooks: Service attribute completeness (individual items vs paragraph)
Seasonal content readiness (60-90d before peak demand)
Review velocity gap (recent reviews vs lifetime totals)
No per-service landing pages (AC vs Heating split)
voiceoverAntipatterns: DO NOT advise switching primary to narrower 'Air conditioning contractor' — limits eligibility for full-HVAC searches.
DO NOT cite seasonal-only review counts without recency context.
DO NOT advise generic CTA copy.
clientOnboardingChecklist: Fill all 15 service attributes as individual items (not paragraphs)
Set 'Open 24 hours' attribute if emergency service offered
Set veteran-owned / women-owned attributes if accurate
Display license + insurance on GBP description + website footer
Upload 20+ photos (seasonal mix — both summer + winter projects)
Set up SMS post-job review request
Build citation profile across 15 targets including manufacturer directories
Add LocalBusiness schema with NAP + service area + bi-seasonal hours
Establish weekly GBP post cadence with seasonal calendar (cooling Mar-Apr, heating Sep-Oct)
Add maintenance plan offering visible on GBP + website
Set up review response process — target ≥85% response rate
Build separate AC vs Heating service-area landing pages per city served
Audit website: HTTPS, schema, <2.5s load, NAP above fold, maintenance CTA

kpiTargets.rankingTimeline: Map Pack 30-60 days; meaningful lead lift 3-5 months

industryNotes: Category + services + attributes account for ~50% of HVAC ranking — biggest leverage point.
Bi-seasonal: cooling demand spikes Jun-Aug, heating spikes Dec-Feb. Content must be indexed 60-90d before.
Maintenance plans are the highest-LTV product in HVAC — visible on profile = trust + retention signal.
Manufacturer certifications (Carrier, Trane, Lennox) unlock additional citation directories.
consumesIn: FUTURE: Brain client-strategy module — HVAC clients need seasonal content calendar
FUTURE: admin onboarding wizard with manufacturer-certification check
FUTURE: portal KPI dashboard with seasonal content readiness gauge

sources: Whitespark 2026 Local Search Ranking Factors Report (Darren Shaw, published Nov 6 2025)
BrightLocal Google Local Algorithm and Ranking Factors (ongoing)
Sterling Sky Breaking Boundaries of GBP Categories

localPackMapsWeights2026.note: Sum exceeds 1.0 because weights overlap (proximity is its own meta-factor). 8 of top 10 individual signals come from GBP.

aiSearchVisibilityWeights2026_NEW.note: NEW category in Whitespark 2026 — tracks rankings in AI search (Google AI Overviews, Perplexity, ChatGPT search). On-page content is the top lever in AI search, NOT GBP.

reviewSignalsTrendline.change: +4 pts; recency + sentiment now outweigh raw count
reviewSignalsTrendline.implication: A business with 200 reviews + none recent ranks BELOW a business with 80 reviews + steady weekly flow

industryResearchDeep.strongestIndividualSignal: Primary GBP category (strongest single ranking factor in Local Pack)

topNegativeRankingFactors2026.note: One of the most damaging issues a business can have. Direct corroboration for the DB-gated categoryServiceVsProduct rule — wrong category advice = #2 worst penalty.

topNegativeRankingFactors2026.factor: Permanently closed flag missing on closed business

topNegativeRankingFactors2026.note: Rankings begin to degrade in the final hour a business is open each day

topNegativeRankingFactors2026.factor: Hidden address for non-SAB (when guideline says to)
topNegativeRankingFactors2026.note: Paradox — following Google's own guideline hurts

keyShiftsSince2023: GBP signals remain dominant at 32% — 8 of top 10 individual Local Pack signals come from GBP
New AI Search Visibility tracking — top lever is on-page content (24%) NOT GBP (12%)
Recency now outweighs review count for ranking
directBenchmarkValidations: Whitespark 2026 confirms our voiceoverAntipattern about category-switching: incorrect primary category is the #2 negative ranking factor (penalty 214).
Whitespark 2026 confirms review velocity (recency) emphasis — recent stream > lifetime total.
Whitespark 2026 confirms business hours weight — locked in voiceoverHooks + commonPitfalls.
